sections.label: Section 12.4
sections.original: ὧν ὥρη μὲν ἀπήμβλυνται , θυμὸς δὲ μενοινᾷ .
sections.translation: whose powers are dulled , but whose hearts still desire .

sections.label: Section 13.1
sections.original: Ἀνδρὸς μὲν στέφανος παῖδες , πύργοι δὲ πόληος ,
sections.translation: Children are a man ’ s crown , towers of a city ;

sections.label: Section 13.2
sections.original: ἵπποι δ ’ αὖ πεδίου κόσμος , νῆες δὲ θαλάσσης ,
sections.translation: horses are the glory of a plain , and so are ships of the sea ;

sections.label: Section 13.3
sections.original: χρήματα δ ’ αὔξει οἶκον , ἀτὰρ γεραροὶ βασιλῆες
sections.translation: wealth will make a house great , and reverend princes

sections.label: Section 13.4
sections.original: ἥμενοι εἰν ἀγορῇ κόσμος λαοῖσιν [*] ὁρᾶσθαι ·
sections.translation: seated in assembly are a goodly sight for the folk to see .

sections.label: Section 13.5
sections.original: αἰθομένου δὲ πυρὸς γεραρώτερος οἶκος ἰδέσθαι
sections.translation: But a blazing fire makes a house look more comely upon a winter ’ s day ,

sections.label: Section 13.6
sections.original: ἤματι χειμερίῳ , ὁπότ ’ ἂν νίφῃσι Κρονίων .
sections.translation: when the Son of Cronos sends down snow .

sections.label: Section 14.1
sections.original: Εἰ μὲν δώσετε μισθὸν ἀείσω , ὦ κεραμῆες .
sections.translation: Potters , if you will give me a reward , I will sing for you .

sections.label: Section 14.2
sections.original: δεῦρ , ἄγ , Ἀθηναίη καὶ ὑπέρσχεθε χεῖρα καμίνου .
sections.translation: Come , then , Athena , with hand upraised [*] over the kiln .

sections.label: Section 14.3
sections.original: εὖ δὲ περανθεῖεν [*] κότυλοι καὶ πάντα κάναστρα
sections.translation: Let the pots and all the dishes turn out well

sections.label: Section 14.4
sections.original: φρυχθῆναί τε καλῶς καὶ τιμῆς ὦνον ἀρέσθαι ,
sections.translation: and be well fired : let them fetch good prices

sections.label: Section 14.5
sections.original: πολλὰ μὲν εἰν ἀγορῇ πωλεύμενα , πολλὰ δ ’ ἀγυιαῖς ,
sections.translation: and be sold in plenty in the market , and plenty in the streets .

sections.label: Section 14.6
sections.original: πολλὰ δὲ κερδῆναι , ἡμῖν δὲ δή , ὥς σφιν ἀεῖσαι .
sections.translation: Grant that the potters may get great gain and grant me so to sing to them .

sections.label: Section 14.7
sections.original: ἢν δ ’ ἐπ ’ ἀναιδείην τρεφθέντες ψεύδε ’ ἄρησθε ,
sections.translation: But if you turn shameless and make false promises ,

sections.label: Section 14.8
sections.original: συγκαλέω δὴ ἔπειτα καμίνων δηλητῆρας ,
sections.translation: then I call together the destroyers of kilns ,

sections.label: Section 14.9
sections.original: Σύντριβ ’ ὁμῶς Σμάραγόν τε καὶ Ἄσβετον ἠδὲ Σαβάκτην
sections.translation: Shatter and Smash and Charr and Crash

sections.label: Section 14.10
sections.original: Ὠμόδαμόν τ , ὃς τῇδε τέχνῃ κακὰ πολλὰ πορίζοι ·
sections.translation: and Crudebake who can work this craft much mischief .

sections.label: Section 14.11
sections.original: πέρθε πυραίθουσαν καὶ δώματα , σὺν δὲ κάμινος
sections.translation: Come all of you and sack the kiln-yard and the buildings : let the

sections.label: Section 14.12
sections.original: πᾶσα κυκηθείη κεραμέων μέγα κωκυσάντων .
sections.translation: whole kiln be shaken up to the potter ’ s loud lament .

sections.label: Section 14.13
sections.original: ὡς γνάθος ἱππείη βρύκει , βρύκοι δὲ κάμινος ,
sections.translation: As a horse ’ s jaw grinds , so let the kiln grind

sections.label: Section 14.14
sections.original: πάντ ’ ἔντοσθ ’ αὐτῆς κεραμήια λεπτὰ ποιοῦσα .
sections.translation: to powder all the pots inside .

sections.label: Section 14.15
sections.original: δεῦρο καὶ Ἠελίου θύγατερ , πολυφάρμακε Κίρκη ,
sections.translation: And you , too , daughter of the Sun , Circe the witch ,

sections.label: Section 14.16
sections.original: ἄγρια φάρμακα βάλλε , κάκου δ ’ αὐτούς τε καὶ ἔργα .
sections.translation: come and cast cruel spells ; hurt both these men and their handiwork .

sections.label: Section 14.17
sections.original: δεῦρο δὲ καὶ Χείρων ἀγέτω πολέας Κενταύρους ,
sections.translation: Let Chiron also come and bring many Centaurs —

sections.label: Section 14.18
sections.original: οἵθ ’ Ἡρακλῆος χεῖρας φύγον οἵτ ’ ἀπόλοντο ,
sections.translation: all that escaped the hands of Heracles and all that were destroyed :

sections.label: Section 14.19
sections.original: τύπτοιεν τάδε ἔργα κακῶς , πίπτοι δὲ κάμινος ·
sections.translation: let them make sad havoc of the pots and overthrow the kiln ,

sections.label: Section 14.20
sections.original: αὐτοὶ δ ’ οἰμώζοντες ὁρῴατο ἔργα πονηρά .
sections.translation: and let the potters see the mischief and be grieved ;

sections.label: Section 14.21
sections.original: γηθήσω δ ’ ὁρόων αὐτῶν κακοδαίμονα τέχνην ·
sections.translation: but I will gloat as I behold their luckless craft .

sections.label: Section 14.22
sections.original: ὃς δέ χ ’ ὑπερκύψῃ , πυρὶ τούτου πᾶν τὸ πρόσωπον
sections.translation: And if anyone of them stoops to peer in , let all his face

sections.label: Section 14.23
sections.original: φλεχθείη , ὡς πάντες ἐπίστωντ ’ αἴσιμα ῥέζειν .
sections.translation: be burned up , that all men may learn to deal honestly .

sections.label: Section 15.1
sections.original: Δῶμα προσετραπόμεσθ ’ ἀνδρὸς μέγα δυναμένοιο ,
sections.translation: [*] Let us betake us to the house of some man of great power ,—

sections.label: Section 15.2
sections.original: ὃς μέγα μὲν δύναται , μέγα δὲ πρέπει [*] ὄλβιος αἰεί .
sections.translation: one who bears great power and is greatly prosperous always .

sections.label: Section 15.3
sections.original: αὐταὶ ἀνακλίνεσθε θύραι · Πλοῦτος γὰρ ἔσεισι
sections.translation: Open of yourselves , you doors , for mighty Wealth will enter in ,

sections.label: Section 15.4
sections.original: πολλός , σὺν Πλούτῳ δὲ καὶ Εὐφροσύνη τεθαλυῖα
sections.translation: and with Wealth comes jolly Mirth

sections.label: Section 15.5
sections.original: Εἰρήνη τ ’ ἀγαθή · ὅσα δ ’ ἄγγεα , μεστὰ μὲν εἴη ,
sections.translation: and gentle Peace . May all the corn-bins be full

sections.label: Section 15.6
sections.original: κυρβαίν δ ’ αἰεὶ κατὰ καρδόπου ἕρποι μᾶζα .
sections.translation: and the mass of dough always overflow the kneading-trough .

sections.label: Section 15.7
sections.original: νῦν μὲν κριθαίην , εὐώπιδα , σησαμόεσσα
sections.translation: Now ( set before us ) cheerful barley-pottage , full of sesame

sections.label: Section 15.8
sections.original: Τοῦ παιδὸς δὲ γυνὴ κατὰ δίφραδα βήσεται ὕμμιν ·
sections.translation: Your son ’ s wife , driving to this house with strong-hoofed mules ,

sections.label: Section 15.9
sections.original: ἡμίονοι δ ’ ἄξουσι κραταίποδες ἐς τόδε δῶμα ·
sections.translation: shall dismount from her carriage to greet you ; [*]

sections.label: Section 15.10
sections.original: αὐτὴ δ ’ ἱστὸν ὑφαίνοι ἐπ ’ ἠλέκτρῳ βεβαυῖα .
sections.translation: may she be shod with golden shoes as she stands weaving at the loom .

sections.label: Section 15.11
sections.original: Νεῦμαί τοι , νεῦμαι ἐνιαύσιος , ὥστε χελιδὼν
sections.translation: I come , and I come yearly , like the swallow

sections.label: Section 15.12
sections.original: ἕστηκ ’ ἐν προθύροις ψιλὴ πόδας · ἀλλὰ φέρ ’ αἶψα
sections.translation: that perches light-footed in the fore-part of your house . But quickly bring

sections.label: Section 15.13
sections.original: πέρσαι τῶ Ἀπόλλωνος γυιάτιδος .

sections.label: Section 16.1
sections.original: Εἰ μέν τι δώσεις · εἰ δὲ μή , οὐχ ἑστήξομεν ·
sections.translation: If you will give us anything ( well ). But if not , we will not wait ,

sections.label: Section 16.2
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ συνοικήσοντες ἐνθάδ ’ ἤλθομεν .
sections.translation: for we are not come here to dwell with you .

sections.label: Section 17.1
sections.original: Ἄνδρες ἄγρης ἁλίης [*] θηρήτορες , ἦ ῥ ’ ἔχομέν τι ;
sections.translation: Hunters of deep sea prey , have we caught anything ?

sections.label: Section 17.2
sections.original: Ὅσσ ’ ἕλομεν , λιπόμεσθ · ὅσα δ ’ οὐχ ἕλομεν φερόμεσθα .
sections.translation: All that we caught we left behind , and all that we did not catch we carry home . [*]

sections.label: Section 17.3
sections.original: Τοίων γὰρ πατέρων ἐξ αἵματος ἐκγεγάασθε ,
sections.translation: Ay , for of such fathers you are sprung

sections.label: Section 17.4
sections.original: οὔτε βαθυκλήρων οὔτ ’ ἄσπετα μῆλα νεμόντων .
sections.translation: as neither hold rich lands nor tend countless sheep .
